resources.string: Clear all remote trust profiles, device tokens, groups, and pending binding codes on this device?
Enter your relay server URL, then save/register this device. Create a binding code when adding this device to a controller.
Tap the code to copy it. It is valid for a short time and can be used by the Web console or another WebHTV device.
File permission is recommended so trust data can be restored after reinstall.
Enter a valid relay server URL
Binding code was not found or expired. Check that both devices use the same relay server, then enter the latest code from the target device.
Register this WebHTV device with the relay. Generate a code when another device needs to manage it.
Enter the binding code shown on another WebHTV device, then manage it from here.
No managed devices. Generate a code on the target device and add it here.
Device group added. Waiting for the target device to sync and come online.
Waiting for target device. If it is offline, the command will run after it reconnects.
Connection failed. Check the service URL, network, or deployment status.
Check failed. Copy diagnostics for details.
Memory relay mode may lose bind codes across devices. Update the Cloudflare Worker Durable Object config before production use.
Choose a local config or enter a URL manually.
No configs on the remote device
Delete this config on the remote device?\n%1$s
No local config of this type
No sites found after parsing this config
Send this device default sync data to %1$s and overwrite related target data?
Sync task created. The source device uploads data first, then the target device restores it.
Cannot sync this device to itself
Sync task failed: missing task ID
You can close this window; sync continues in the background. Reopen it to view recent progress.
Waiting for this device to export data...
Still waiting for completion; this can time out when the target is offline or remote trust is disabled
QR scan is unavailable on this device
Scanned URL filled. Tap Done to save.
This will save the relay URL directly on the scanned device.
Relay URL saved on the other device
Failed to save on the other device: %1$s
Controls remote sync and webhook reporting.
Allow crawler HTTP API to write or delete local viewing progress. Reading current playback is still available when off.
Endpoint URL must start with http

Webhook reporting sends your viewing record, including title and progress, to the server you configure.\n\nThe app does not collect or forward this data to other parties. You can disable or delete endpoints at any time. If a server token is set, it is sent in the X-WebHTV-Token header so your server can group and verify reports.

string-array.name: playback_webhook_field_descriptions
string-array.item: Local vod configuration id. Not stable across devices.
Stable vod configuration key, used to separate different interfaces.
App history key, usually siteKey + vodId.
Player state, such as playing, paused, or ended.
Whether this item is considered completed.
Original episode URL. May contain source-site parameters.
Client flavor, such as mobile or leanback.
Hashed device id. Use only for trusted services.

resources.string: No extensions yet. Tap Add to create a local script or add an external source.
No WebHome sites in current VOD config
Extension source saved and preview refresh requested
Source or code cannot be empty

After enabling, this extension can call the WebHome SDK.

Name: %1$s
Source: %2$s
Match: %3$s
Click an element in the Web tab to inspect it
No requests captured for this filter
Select a request to view details.
Site injection needs file access permission.
Raw JSON: full registry, sites/lives arrays, or one site/live object
Paste full config, proxy snippet, rule array, or one rule object
No device found. Keep both devices on the same LAN with WebHTV open, then refresh.
Waiting for the selected device to send data...
One external-storage relative path per line. TV, TVBox and TVData are synced by default. You can add local package folders; folder contents are synced with Jar/script saved data.
Learn cookie and login-state file paths for one-key sync. %1$d paths are selected. Use Manage paths to review candidates and inspect file contents.
Learning is active. Log in to the drive account, return here, then finish learning. %1$d paths are currently selected.
Login state learning finished: %1$d selected, %2$d pending
Pending candidates and latest findings cleared
No login state paths selected · %1$d pending
%1$d pending items are shown in the tree
This device does not support the required DRM scheme
An unexpected error occurred.\nSorry for the inconvenience.

Debug log service is running.

LAN URL:
%1$s

Local URL:
%2$s

If this TV has no browser, open the LAN URL from a computer or phone on the same network.
No browser app found. Use the LAN URL from another device.

Management page is running.

LAN URL:
%1$s

Local URL:
%2$s

Keep this page open while managing files or settings. The service stops automatically after the browser page is closed for a while, or you can stop it manually.
No browser app found. Use the LAN URL from another device.

This device may restrict network access after the app moves to the background, which can make the browser management page unreachable. Open settings only tries known background running or battery management pages for this device brand. If the system does not allow a direct page, use this manual path:\n\n%1$s
No direct background settings page was found. Use the manual path shown in the dialog.

vivo/iQOO: Settings > Battery > Background power consumption management / High background power consumption > find this app > set it to allow high background power usage. If the direct page fails, search system Settings for Background power consumption management.
Xiaomi/Redmi/POCO: Settings > Apps > Permissions > Autostart > allow this app. Then set this app battery usage to unrestricted.
OPPO/realme/OnePlus: Settings > Apps > Autostart or associated launch > allow this app. Then allow background running in Battery > App battery management.
Huawei/Honor: Phone Manager or Settings > App launch > this app > disable automatic management, then allow autostart, secondary launch, and background running.
Samsung: Settings > Apps > this app > Battery > Unrestricted. Also make sure this app is not in sleeping/deep sleeping apps.
Meizu: Phone Manager > Permission/background management > this app > allow background running. Then set this app to unrestricted in power management.
Settings > Apps > this app > Battery > choose unrestricted or not optimized. If autostart/background running options exist, allow them for this app.
